faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Gowanus apartments and homes?
faqs.answer: Silverfish thrive in Gowanus due to the area's high humidity levels, especially near the Gowanus Canal, and the abundance of older buildings with basement moisture issues. Converted industrial lofts and ground-floor apartments often have the damp, dark conditions silverfish prefer, along with access to paper, cardboard, and organic materials they feed on.
